questions.scenario: The Orders API runs on Cloud Run and reads one database secret while serving requests. Which identity should carry that runtime access?

options.text: The Cloud Run runtime service account for the Orders API.
Every user in the company domain.

questions.explanation: Production app code should run as a workload identity. The runtime service account gets the access needed while the app serves requests.

questions.scenario: A CI/CD pipeline deploys Cloud Run revisions and shifts traffic. The app runtime only reads a secret and writes receipts. What is the healthy identity split?

options.text: Use a deploy identity for releases and a separate runtime service account for the app.
Use the runtime service account for all deployment administration by default.
Use a developer's personal ADC file in production.
Use the same broad Owner identity everywhere to avoid review.

questions.explanation: Deploy and runtime are different jobs. Separating them limits the power available to running application code.

questions.scenario: Which statements about service accounts are correct?

options.text: A service account is an identity for software or automation.
A service account can receive IAM roles.
A service account can appear in audit evidence.
A service account should always share a human's password.

questions.explanation: Service accounts are workload principals. They should not depend on human passwords.
